title: Boat Rental in Cork Harbour: Your Guide to Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
If you are planning a day on Ireland's second-largest natural harbour, boat hire Cork Harbour offers a memorable escape from Cork city. You can rent a motorboat from Crosshaven Boatyard or join a guided rib tour for around €250 to €1,800 per day. Browse verified options on BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cork Harbour?

Prices range from €50 per person for a shared rib tour to €1,800 for a private crewed motor yacht for a full day. A half-day self-drive motorboat (up to 6 guests) costs roughly €250-€400, while a full-day catamaran charter for 12 people sits around €900-€1,300. To compare real-time availability and prices, check boat hire Cork Harbour listings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cork Harbour?

Generally, a basic ICC or equivalent is required for self-drive motorboats over a certain power threshold. Many operators offer a short briefing and a practical test; some smaller ribs require no licence. Always confirm with the owner on BoatHire24, as requirements vary by vessel and insurer. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cork Harbour or list your own.

Ask the owner about local wind patterns: the south-easterly sea breeze strengthens around midday, so plan morning passages for calm conditions inside the harbour.

Where to set off in Cork Harbour

Most rentals depart from Crosshaven, with its sheltered marina and direct access to the harbour mouth. Cobh offers a deep-water pontoon near the heritage centre, ideal for larger yachts. Monkstown Bay and Ringaskiddy have smaller slipways for trailer boats. Further east, Roches Point marks the entrance to the open sea, while Spike Island and Haulbowline are popular stop-offs for lunch and history.

Which boat to rent

  1. RIB ( Rigid Inflatable Boat), Best for speed and sightseeing, carries 6-8 guests, from €50 per person for a 90-minute tour.
  2. Motorboat (4-6 guests), Ideal for a family picnic exploring the harbour’s inlets, from €250 for half a day.
  3. Catamaran (up to 12 guests), Stable and spacious for a relaxed full-day cruise, from €900 with a skipper.
  4. Motor Yacht (6-8 guests), Offers cabin comfort and a flying bridge; see the Fairline Targa 43 listing for a sample of a well-equipped 43-footer.
  5. Sailing Yacht (4-6 guests), Perfect for those wanting a quieter experience under sail, from €350 half-day.

Cost breakdown for boat hire in Cork Harbour

  • Shared rib tour: €50-€80 per person, 1.5 hours, includes lifejackets and guide.
  • Self-drive motorboat half-day: €250-€400, 4 hours, fuel extra, up to 6 guests.
  • Skppered catamaran full-day: €900-€1,300, 8 hours, can accommodate up to 12.
  • Private crewed motor yacht day: €1,400-€1,800, 8 hours, includes skipper, fuel and refreshments.

Best season for boating in Cork Harbour

High season runs from May to September, with July and August being warmest and busiest. The prevailing south-westerly wind can bring rain, but the harbour is sheltered. In 2026, consider visiting in June for the Cork Harbour Festival. Winter charters are limited, although some ribs operate year-round for fishing trips.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50 per person to €1,800 per boat per day
  • Peak months: May-September
  • Typical trip length: 1.5 to 8 hours
  • Recommended max capacity: 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Cork Harbour

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Cork Harbour?

Yes, some operators provide a short briefing and test drive for boats with a smaller engine, or you can join a guided rib tour where no licence is needed.

What is the best way to get to Cork Harbour from the city centre?

Cork city centre is about 20 minutes by car to Crosshaven, and regular bus services run from the station. Taxis cost around €30-€40.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a boat in Cork Harbour?

Fuel is often extra for self-drive boats, and you may need to pay a security deposit, typically €300-€500, which is refunded after the trip.

Can I rent a boat for a full week in Cork Harbour?

Yes, a few operators offer weekly charters on larger yachts, but availability is limited; early booking through BoatHire24 is recommended.

Boat owners can reach thousands of holidaymakers by listing their vessel on BoatHire24, the simplest way to earn from your boat when you are not using it.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cork Harbour and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
